whatIsBitcoin.question: What exactly is Bitcoin?
whatIsBitcoin.title: So what exactly is Bitcoin?
whatIsBitcoin.text: Bitcoin is digital money that works without banks or governments, secured by cryptography and a global network of computers.
feedback: Correct! Bitcoin is decentralized digital money.
Incorrect! Bitcoin is not a video game.
Nope! Bitcoin is not a cartoon character.
Incorrect! Bitcoin is not a credit card.

whoCreated.question: Who created Bitcoin?
whoCreated.title: The mysterious creator
whoCreated.text: Bitcoin was created by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.
feedback: Correct! Satoshi Nakamoto published the Bitcoin whitepaper in 2008.
Incorrect! Elon Musk did not create Bitcoin.
Nope! Mark Zuckerberg did not create Bitcoin.
Incorrect! Bill Gates did not create Bitcoin.

maxSupply.question: What is the maximum supply of Bitcoin?
maxSupply.title: Bitcoin's hard cap
maxSupply.text: Bitcoin has a fixed maximum supply of 21 million coins, making it scarce by design.
feedback: Correct! Only 21 million bitcoin will ever exist.
Incorrect! It's not 100 million.
Nope! Bitcoin has a fixed supply, not unlimited.
Incorrect! It's not 1 billion.

whatIsBlockchain.question: What is the blockchain?
whatIsBlockchain.title: The public ledger
whatIsBlockchain.text: The blockchain is a public, distributed ledger that records every Bitcoin transaction in chronological blocks.
feedback: Correct! It's the shared record of all transactions.
Incorrect! A wallet app is not the blockchain.
Nope! It's not a social media platform.
Incorrect! It's not a mining company.

whatIsMining.question: What is Bitcoin mining?
whatIsMining.title: Securing the network
whatIsMining.text: Mining is the process of using computing power to validate transactions and secure the network, earning new bitcoin as a reward.
answers: Validating transactions to secure the network
feedback: Correct! Miners secure the network and validate transactions.
Incorrect! There's no physical digging involved.
Nope! Bitcoin has no physical bills.
Incorrect! Mining is not hacking.

privateKey.question: What does a private key control?
privateKey.title: Your secret access
privateKey.text: A private key gives you control and ownership over the bitcoin associated with your wallet address.
answers: Access to and control of your bitcoin
feedback: Correct! Private keys control your bitcoin.
Incorrect! It's not your internet password.
Nope! Not related to your email account.
Incorrect! Not your phone's lock screen.

notYourKeys.question: What does "not your keys, not your coins" mean?
notYourKeys.title: A core Bitcoin principle
notYourKeys.text: If you don't control your private keys, you don't truly own your bitcoin — you're trusting a third party.
answers: If you don't hold your keys, you don't truly own your bitcoin
You should write your keys on social media
Exchanges always protect your coins forever
feedback: Correct! Self custody means holding your own keys.
Incorrect! Never share your keys publicly.
Nope! Exchanges can fail or be hacked.
Incorrect! Keys are central to security.

hardwareWallet.question: What is the main benefit of a hardware wallet?
hardwareWallet.title: Cold storage security
hardwareWallet.text: A hardware wallet keeps your private keys offline, protecting them from online hackers and malware.
answers: Keeps private keys offline and secure
Lets you spend faster than cash
feedback: Correct! Hardware wallets offer cold storage security.
Incorrect! Speed isn't the main benefit.
Nope! It doesn't connect to social media.
Incorrect! Hardware wallets don't mine.

seedPhrase.question: What should you do with your seed phrase?
seedPhrase.title: Protecting your backup
seedPhrase.text: Your seed phrase is the master backup to your wallet and should be kept offline, private, and secure.
answers: Keep it offline and private, never share it
feedback: Correct! Never share your seed phrase with anyone.
Incorrect! Posting it online is extremely dangerous.
Nope! Never email your seed phrase.
Incorrect! Real support never asks for seed phrases.

halving.question: What happens during a Bitcoin halving?
halving.title: Cutting the issuance
halving.text: Approximately every four years, the reward miners receive for new blocks is cut in half, slowing the issuance of new bitcoin.
answers: The mining reward is cut in half
feedback: Correct! Block rewards halve roughly every 4 years.
Incorrect! Price isn't guaranteed to change instantly.
Nope! Supply does not double.
Incorrect! Transactions aren't free forever.

inflation.question: Why is Bitcoin often called "digital gold"?
inflation.title: A scarce store of value
inflation.text: Like gold, Bitcoin is scarce, durable, and not controlled by any government, making it attractive as a store of value.
answers: It's scarce and acts as a store of value like gold
feedback: Correct! Scarcity makes Bitcoin similar to gold.
Incorrect! Bitcoin is purely digital, no physical gold.
Nope! Bitcoin isn't jewelry.
Incorrect! No government prints bitcoin.
